figures.caption: Figure 4: Multi-head PINN architecture used in the offline stage. A shared body produces features Hθ(s) and each head corresponds to a linear output layer with weights Wk, k = 1, . . . ,K.

Figure 1: ODE1 equation 14. Left: overlay of one-shot transfer predictions uTL(t) (solid) and numerical reference solutions uref(t) (dashed) for a representative subset of test instances. Right: mean solution discrepancy over the same subset of test instances (red line indicates ∆(t) = 0).

Figure 2: ODE2 equation 15. Left: overlay of one-shot transfer predictions uTL(t) (solid) and numerical reference solutions uref(t) (dashed) for a representative subset of test instances. Right: mean solution discrepancy over the same subset of test instances (red line indicates ∆(t) = 0).

Figure 3: PDE1 equation 16. Left: predicted field uTL(x, t) on the [0, 1]×[0, 1] space–time domain. Right: pointwise squared error ( uTL(x, t)− utrue(x, t) )2 against the manufactured solution on the same grid.
